message:
perf(pager): collapse seqlock recheck on published-read fast path

`SimpleTransaction::get_page` already calls `PublishedPagerState::snapshot()`
once at the top of the published-read loop and then again — purely as the
seqlock recheck — to confirm the page-plane read isn't torn. The recheck
only consumes `confirm.snapshot_gen` while `snapshot()` reads six other
metadata atomics (visible_commit_seq, db_size, journal_mode,
freelist_count, checkpoint_active, page_set_size).

Add a `current_sequence_gen()` method that does just the single Acquire
load on `sequence` and substitute it at both confirmation sites
(zero-fill-read and try_get_page success). Seqlock semantics are
unchanged: if the recheck equals the recorded even snapshot_gen, no
writer started AND finished a publish during the page read; an odd
in-progress value can't equal an even start, so it correctly forces the
retry/wait branch.

Adds a focused unit test pinning the contract that
`current_sequence_gen()` agrees with `snapshot().snapshot_gen` before and
after a publish, advances on commit, and stays even at quiescence —
drift between the two would silently break the seqlock recheck.

Hot path is the most common cached read — at 1t each `get_page` paid
seven atomic loads twice; this collapses the second reading to one.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
